task_completion.description: Open the pytracer project in an IDE or text editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An editor or IDE (like PyCharm) is open showing the directory tree for /home/ga/PycharmProjects/pytracer.

task_completion.description: Modify the renderer code to fix the math/logic bugs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code editor shows active modifications to mathematical formulas or logic related to Field of View, Sphere intersection roots, reflection vectors, or shadow acne (e.g., adding a small epsilon to intersection points).

task_completion.description: Execute the test suite in the tests/ directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or IDE test runner shows a command being executed to run the tests (e.g., pytest tests/ or python -m unittest).

task_completion.description: Verify that all 4 tests pass successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The test runner or terminal output explicitly displays that exactly 4 tests have passed (e.g., '4 passed' in green text).

task_completion.description: Run the raytracer to generate the output image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal shows the main script being executed, or the file explorer/IDE shows output/render.png has been newly created or updated.

task_completion.description: View the generated render.png to ensure visual artifacts are fixed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image viewer or IDE displays render.png. The image should show a cleanly rendered 320x180 scene with correct reflections, undistorted objects, and smooth shadows without 'shadow acne' (black speckles/noise on surfaces).

integrity.description: Ensure tests were actually run through a testing framework.
integrity.visual_evidence: Test output should look like standard pytest or unittest output, not a hardcoded 'print("4 passed")' or a text file.

integrity.description: Ensure the image was generated by the script, not downloaded or copied.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal history should show the Python script being executed to produce the image, with no commands like wget, curl, or cp fetching a pre-made image.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent modified the actual project files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path in the editor should clearly point to /home/ga/PycharmProjects/pytracer/ and not a temporary or mock directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project directory is /home/ga/PycharmProjects/pytracer. The agent must fix 4 bugs and pass exactly 4 tests in the tests/ directory. The final rendered image must be saved to output/render.png. The rendered image is 320x180 pixels and should be visually clean, without shadow acne (speckles) or distorted reflections.
